content.text: mira's bedtime window is 7:30 pm, and the routine you've settled on is bath → story → lights out — a solid three-step sequence that your memory shows has been running consistently. that order works well because the warm bath nudges core body temperature down (which the **Hale Toddler Sleep Framework** flags as one of the most reliable biological wind-down cues at this stage), and the story gives her a calm, predictable bridge between the active bath and darkness — toddlers at 36 months respond especially well to that kind of narrative anchor before sleep. one optional nudge: if you notice she's been cranky by evening after a short nap (which has come up a few times recently), starting the bath a touch earlier — say 7:10 or 7:15 — on those days can help you beat the overtired window before it closes.
